description:
Goal
Close activation partitioning only after the implemented model is documented, observable, and mechanically protected. After this ticket, `ActivationKey` means identity partition, `ActivationInputs` means joined body evidence, and cyclic semantic ascent is activation-dataflow-shaped rather than `Recursive(function)`-shaped.

Signal
- `.agent/docs/fact-engine.md`, `.agent/docs/semantic-fixpoint.md`, `.agent/docs/type-specialization.md`, `.agent/docs/type-world.md`, telemetry docs, and any guide/northstar-facing text describe the code's actual model: current vs settled fact uses, activation identity partition, joined input evidence, dynamic activation-cycle convergence, stored-state semantic publication, and meaningful-event budgets.
- Grep-level checks prove stale concepts are gone or explicitly ticketed: `convergence_class`, recursive-only key widening, `ActivationKey` as body input, raw closure-capture key defaults, `Recursive(function)` as the semantic ascent switch, `retain_stored_analysis_outputs` or equivalent retained-output workaround naming, missing return as `none`, unresolved return as `any`, `ReturnType` as readiness, stale `FactValue::Inputs` docs, debug telemetry payload budgets, and unbounded `fz2 --log-telemetry` workflows.
- Quicksort and Enum.to_list/map convergence telemetry budgets are documented with measured post-fix numbers and hard-timeout commands.
- `cargo test --lib compiler2:: -- --test-threads=1` passes under a hard external timeout, plus the focused quicksort/Enum/dynamic-cycle/stored-publication tests introduced by `.9.7` through `.9.9`.
- No temporary construction fixtures, ignored convergence tests, or local affordances from `.9.5` through `.9.9` remain without a new blocking ticket.

Strategy
- Update docs only after `.9.5` through `.9.9` are implemented and verified; docs must cite the implemented model, not the intended future.
- Replace stale prose instead of layering caveats. If the code has changed, delete the old model wording.
- Run no-go searches as part of closeout; every survivor is either deleted here or becomes a new blocker before `fz-rh2.18.6` starts.
- Reconcile any target/plan docs that still describe bridge/shim/scaffold work as acceptable; if a future pull-product epic is needed, ticket it explicitly instead of hiding it in activation-partition docs.
- Keep telemetry compact: budget against meaningful semantic/artifact movement, not job span payload dumps.

Outro
No stale activation-key/body-input docs, no renamed `convergence_class`, no recursive-only convergence switch, no compatibility shim preserving old key semantics, no transient retained-output patch, no missing/unresolved evidence encoded as type top/bottom, no leftover ignored convergence tests, no temporary telemetry budget that ignores event explosions, and no bridge/scaffold language presented as the accepted path for this arc.
title: Compiler2 activation partitioning closeout: docs and deletion contract
